title: The Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS)
summary: This Treasury page provides general information about CFIUS, an interagency committee that reviews foreign investments in the United States for national security implications. It summarizes recent regulatory actions including a February 2026 RFI on the Known Investor Program, November 2024 final rules on penalties and military installation definitions, and historical information about FIRRMA and Executive Order 14083. The page is an informational hub rather than a market-moving document.
key_points: CFIUS operates under Section 721 of the Defense Production Act of 1950, as amended, with Treasury serving as chair
A February 6, 2026 RFI seeks public input on the Known Investor Program and streamlining the foreign investment review process, with comments closing March 18, 2026
The Known Investor Program (KIP) pilot aims to facilitate investment from allies by collecting information from eligible foreign investors in advance of filings
November 2024 Final Rule amended penalty provisions, mitigation agreement negotiation, and information request procedures, effective December 26, 2024
November 2024 Final Rule added 59 military installations to CFIUS jurisdiction, removed 3, and expanded jurisdiction around 8 existing installations, effective December 9, 2024
Executive Order 14083 (September 15, 2022) elaborated on national security factors CFIUS considers when reviewing transactions
why_it_matters: This source is a Treasury informational page about CFIUS procedures and regulatory history. It does not contain market-moving macro catalysts or market structure analysis.
